steps.title: Translate the first condition
steps.content: "Connor has 4 times as many dollars as Maria" means Connor's amount equals 4 times Maria's: \(c = 4m\).

steps.title: Translate the second condition
steps.content: "Together they have a total of $25.00" means \(c + m = 25\).

steps.title: Eliminate choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '\(c=4m\) and \(c+m=25\)': Matches both translations exactly. ✓
  • Choice B — '\(m=4c\) and \(c+m=25\)': \(m=4c\) would mean Maria has 4 times Connor's amount, reversing the relationship. ✗
  • Choice C — '\(c=25m\) and \(c+m=4\)': Swaps the roles of 4 and 25; total would be $4, not $25. ✗
  • Choice D — '\(m=25c\) and \(c+m=4\)': Both equations are wrong — wrong multiplier and wrong total. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
